result:
**Reviewing repo state and skills**I’m reviewing the existing EPAM-015/016/017 implementation first so I can verify the current behavior, identify any gaps against EPAM-018, and then patch only what’s actually missing.The enterprise features are already present, so I’m narrowing this to correctness and isolation issues rather than building from scratch. Next I’m reading the constraint, consultation, and auditor paths together with their tests to see where the guarantees are weak.**Inspecting shared options mutation**I’ve identified two likely weak spots already: consultation is implemented by monkey-patching `AgentRunner.prototype.run`, and block constraints are appended after the base prompt instead of being structurally forced to the absolute front. I’m checking the runner construction path now to fix those guarantees without destabilizing the rest of the session flow.**Investigating constraint loading****Planning constraint loader integration****Inspecting package scripts****Searching for tests****Assessing provider interface thread safety****Assessing consultation integration****Assessing command registration need****Identifying missing command tests****Inspecting agent runner types****Identifying projectId helper source****Analyzing cache and state leakage risks****Evaluating consultation command feasibility**The consultation path is weaker than it first looked: the command exists but isn’t registered, and as a standalone process it currently only queues in-memory state that disappears on exit. I’m reading the EPAM-017 shape from the repo and then I’ll wire consultation into an actual persisted next-turn flow instead of relying on a runtime monkey patch.**Planning cross-process consult persistence****Clarifying constraint loading scope and helper integration****Planning test additions and refactoring****Designing consultation storage API****Designing transient user message handling****Refining prompt assembly and auditor message handling****Creating session constraints module****Planning unit tests for async consultation****Planning comprehensive unit tests**I’ve got the concrete fixes scoped now. I’m updating four areas: make constraints load into actual session startup and force block constraints to the absolute front, remove the consultation monkey patch and replace it with a one-turn persisted injection that does not contaminate saved history, tighten auditor gate semantics and add explicit isolation tests, then run vitest and `tsc --noEmit`.**Planning multi-file patch and helper****Splitting patch into smaller chunks****Inspecting AgentRunner duplicate logic**I hit a patch conflict in `AgentRunner`, so I’m applying the changes in smaller slices to avoid clobbering surrounding work. The intended behavior is unchanged: separate transient model input from persisted conversation history.**Inspecting agent runner code****Updating REPL behavior****Considering patch for legacy function references****Reviewing AgentRunner message handling****Planning comprehensive test additions****Considering new unit test need**I’m on the test pass now. The main additions are around the failure modes the original stories missed: malformed constraint payloads, one-turn consultation persistence without history bleed, and auditor gating/isolation behavior under parallel execution.**Planning command test additions****Planning typecheck integration**Vitest rejected the `--runInBand` flag on this repo’s version, so I’m rerunning the tests with plain file targets. Typecheck is still running in parallel; once that finishes I’ll fix any compile issues before the full verification pass.**Confirming type safety, planning tests****Running full test suite and typecheck**The focused enterprise-phase suite is clean and `tsc --noEmit` is clean. I’m running the full Vitest suite now to catch any regressions from the consultation API and prompt-loading changes outside the directly touched paths.**Inspecting sync test import issue****Patching tests to match current config****Adjusting test setup for temp config****Investigating sync code writes****Investigating missing file writes in tests****Analyzing test failure causes****Adjusting test mocks and assertions****Adjusting test expectations for console output****Running npm test command**The last pass is just regression verification now: full Vitest suite and a fresh typecheck together. If both land clean, I’ll append the KB note and close with the specific enterprise-phase guarantees that were fixed and verified.**Planning poll typecheck session****Reviewing knowledge base entries****Appending KB entry for consultation bleed**I found one genuinely non-obvious pattern worth recording: one-turn prompt injection can still bleed across later turns if you store the injected text in the persisted conversation history, even when you “consume” the pending flag correctly. I’m appending that as the KB-004 note now.**Considering git diff summary****Composing final concise summary**Implemented EPAM-018 and verified the enterprise phase end to end.

The main fixes are in [ContextBuilder.ts](/home/bjerome/projects/ai/epam-cli/src/context/ContextBuilder.ts), [AgentRunner.ts](/home/bjerome/projects/ai/epam-cli/src/agent/AgentRunner.ts), and [sessionPrompt.ts](/home/bjerome/projects/ai/epam-cli/src/constraints/sessionPrompt.ts). Block constraints are now forced to the absolute front of the system prompt, remote constraints are actually loaded at session start for `chat`/`run`/REPL, malformed constraint payloads are rejected by Zod without crashing, and constraint caching is isolated per project in [ConstraintLoader.ts](/home/bjerome/projects/ai/epam-cli/src/constraints/ConstraintLoader.ts).

Consultation was reworked so it is truly single-turn. The `consult` command is now registered in [index.ts](/home/bjerome/projects/ai/epam-cli/src/cli/index.ts), queued state is persisted instead of relying on a process-local monkey patch, and the injected consultation text is sent only as transient model input so it does not bleed into saved session history or later turns. That path is covered in [ConsultationContext.test.ts](/home/bjerome/projects/ai/epam-cli/test/unit/context/ConsultationContext.test.ts) and [AgentContext.test.ts](/home/bjerome/projects/ai/epam-cli/test/unit/agent/AgentContext.test.ts).

Auditor gating was tightened in [AuditorRunner.ts](/home/bjerome/projects/ai/epam-cli/src/auditors/AuditorRunner.ts) so `[SEVERITY: info] No findings.` does not trip thresholds, and new tests verify persona injection, threshold behavior, toggle behavior, and parallel isolation in [AuditorRunner.test.ts](/home/bjerome/projects/ai/epam-cli/test/unit/auditors/AuditorRunner.test.ts) and [AuditorRegistry.test.ts](/home/bjerome/projects/ai/epam-cli/test/unit/auditors/AuditorRegistry.test.ts). I also updated stale test expectations in the sync/config suites and appended KB-004 to [KB.md](/home/bjerome/projects/ai/epam-cli/orchestrations/agents/KB.md).

Verification: `npm test` passed with 196/196 tests, and `npm run typecheck` passed with `tsc --noEmit` clean.
